item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Normally I would edit my art into my author post, but that is, of course, themed, and I don't want to mess with it. So…

I made some art!
Ghost light...

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks for the compliment!

Also, hmm… interesting find.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Some things I've noticed that I haven't seen anyone discussing:

  • How 'bout them image files, eh? The ones that aren't in the article itself.
  • There are two edits in the History with future dates on them that include new information.

I'm having a hard time piecing it all together myself, but I'm surprised to see only one other reference in the discussion to the unique edits in the History and nothing about the extra images in the Files.

Also, this is profoundly creepy.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hmm… interesting find…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: By objects being converted into props, do you mean, like, non-functional replicas? In my work in theater (disclaimer: I worked in costumes, which is props-adjacent), if an item needed for a prop was not too expensive to acquire, and not too dangerous to use on stage, we just used the real thing. Or, are objects in the active area transformed into different objects, relevant to the narrative? More shocking, perhaps, would be if the environs were transformed into set pieces, if that's an image that's interesting to you.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Not liking the usage of plural pronouns to refer to PoI-3713 when it was established that she was female.

Also, I noticed some edits in the history marked off by dates. Are those part of the narrative?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"Inside my heart is breaking
My makeup may be flaking
But my smile still stays on…"

This is far more disturbing than any cold posted Thing What Kills You could ever be. As someone who spent a lot of time backstage (as well as onstage) in high school, I really appreciated the quiet horror.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
When the world is about to end, all humanity will die, with their personalities and memories replaced by actors who originally created them. Everyone in the entire world will face towards a single direction in the 4th dimension, and bow. Applause will be heard through the cosmos, as the curtains close.

This kinda feels like that idea I used to have. It's a bit more apocalyptic, but yknow. Something tangible and physical, yet pataphysical? Maybe worth looking into some connection to the Jim Look SCP?

Loved this SCP!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Effing Wikidot making double posts.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is all one anomaly, but the way in which Camila interacts with it makes us see it from a different angle. I think that’s all I feel at will to say. Thank you for reading!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Quick question, I'll try to keep as unspoilery as possible:

Was what was already happening to PoI related to the original SCP-3713 phenomena or is this a case of two unrelated but similarly "themed" anomalies crashing in to one another?

3713 had been going on for over 100 years. I thought maybe the events were "auditions" but apparently PoI had been experiencing the other anomaly since way before her town got hit?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Actually, I will say, I’m glad someone was worried for her. When writing that interview with Camila, I wasn’t going for spook (well, maybe somewhat), I was really aiming for a poor, horribly damaged girl. When writing that interview, I felt really bad for her, not really scared about what she said. I tried to write Dorer’s understanding and humane manner to reflect that feeling even more. Glad you liked the whole thing!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It didn't personally pull me in until the focus on the PoI (I wasn't so much as disturbed as I was worried for her), but man did it pay off. The ending made me very upset that

Spoiler
Hide: Spoiler

despite the Foundation's promise, Ms. Marquez will probably never truly see her child again, as implied by the "[…]ever".

Thanks for the performance!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I dig it. I do have one minor gripe, though. Throughout the article certain individuals are referred to by the third-person plural pronoun (they) rather than a singular pronoun (he, she, it.) In at least one section an individual seems to be referred to by both singular and plural interchangeably. Overall it didn't really detract from the events portrayed, but each time my attention was drawn to it rather than the events in the moment of reading.

Other than that, I found the article to be thoroughly disturbing. Well done!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Dr. Dorer: Yes, it's real. Just as real as you or me. I can open it if you want to see.

I thought he was about to break out into song here!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This was a light and funny article until it very much wasn’t.

+1, hope to see more

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh boy.

I don't usually comment on anything but man, that was the most frightening existential horror, and the most interesting characters I've seen in a while. The twist is marvellous too.

This just kept surpassing my expectations all the way through. Bravo.

I assume there will be a lot of ovations for this performance.

Oh and also, in case I don't see ya, good afternoon, good evening, and good night!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Because another GoI (like the Globalition of occult) might do something than the anomaly itself?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: i confess i've always wanted the super power to make busby berkley musicals occur in real life so this was an easy sell for me, but then you went and wrote a damn fine skip. thanks, and encore!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The show must go on!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Encore?

C a n d o

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Okay so

that was incredible. At first I thought this was just a fun little SCP where people are compelled to sing and perform in musicals. That in it of itself is worth a +1, but the stuff with the PoI was *so* compelling! I loved reading it. This is what a great SCP looks like. Bravo. Bravisimo. Encore!

Also, I like the little button to close the interviews. I've never seen that before. That was convenient.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Much as I always say life would be better if it had musical numbers, I found myself thinking this was a little silly at first. But you convinced me with the descriptions of the various musicals that happened. (I want to hear "We Stay in the Dark (So You May Live in the Light)" now!) And then you get into the girl who broke the whole thing, and that was just really intense and emotional, solid work, the kind of thing that I keep coming back here for.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ayy, it's up! Nice work at creating some fucking weird stuff.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Question is, is 3713 satisfied now? Will it come back?

Well, we wouldn't want to keep an audience waiting, now, would we?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
What do you think?

Me, personally, I think the show must go on…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
For the sake of PoI-3713's mental health her mother, Cecelia Marquez, has been made somewhat aware of the events that transpired and the consequences of sharing information

I loved this portion. The Foundation actually reveals itself to someone in an effort to make things better. But I could easily see the knowledge of this event making things worse.

This entire thing is fantastic. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is fucking disturbing. I love it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Goddamn, the twist at the end is just upsetting in the best way possible.

Question is, is 3713 satisfied now? Will it come back?

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's finally arrived! I see it's been tightened up since the last time I saw it, which is good, since it's a pretty lengthy read as-is. The pacing works a lot better now, though, and I love the ending you guys went with. I'm still not sold on the containment breach event, but nevertheless, this is definitely upvote worthy.

item.title: ACT II
item.content:encoded:
VARAXOUS enters stage right
Why should we let them sing?
VARAXOUS: Greetings all! Wonderful turnout we have here.
So absorbed in their cheers and jeers.
Applause
Have you ever thought about why it sings?
VARAXOUS: Thank you, thank you! Now, as much as I would love to take all of the credit, all I did was add some words and fight some owls for a good dream. While Darkstuff was an absolutely crucial piece of the puzzle, without the wonderful help of everyone involved, this wouldn't have had turned nearly as amazing as it did. I'd like to thank Tanhony, Weryllium, CaptainKirby, Ninevolt, thebillith, not_a_seagull, and PeppersGhost. Without their help, there wouldn't be any lights, any set, hell, not even any actors! So please give them, and everyone else involved in this wonderful production, a big hand!
USE YOUR EYES AND LOOK UP. TRULY SEE.
Pause to wait for the applause to subside
The owl's view is so far and so wide.
VARAXOUS: Now I'm sure you're all tired of the sweaty, skinny person standing in front of you all preventing the play from starting. So without further adieu, let the play…
That sometimes they forget to check their own feathers.
VARAXOUS takes a large bow
A dream plucked away from one of the 12.
VARAXOUS: …Begin!
What lies in the center?
Curtains fall in front of VARAXOUS and every light turns off except for the ones focused on the stage.
theykeeponimprovingtheykeeponrevising
justletmehideaway
DON'T
STOP
LOOKING

item.title: ACT I
item.content:encoded:
DARKSTUFF enters stage left
letmeoutofhere
DARKSTUFF : Hello! Thank you for coming to see our performance! I hope you will enjoy the show, and I will remind you to silence your cellphones now.
iwasnevermeanttoperform
Pause
thereissomethingbehindthedoor
DARKSTUFF : There will be an intermission halfway through the show, and you will find exits to… well…
thereissomethingbehindthewalls
Pause for audience laughter
thereissomethingbehindmyhead
DARKSTUFF : You might just want to sit tight, then! This thing has been in production for roughly five months now, and I hope that the effort shows. The pictures — and videos — were taken by myself, and so was the original idea. However, I must admit that this play would have been unfortunately plotless if it weren't for my pal. Please, welcome, the one! The only! VARAXOUS!
theyputmeinthefiles
DARKSTUFF motions stage right, as VARAXOUS enters
